meta_title: Impact Doors in Lauderhill, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Lauderhill, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, $0 down financing. Protect your home & lower insurance costs.
h1: Impact Doors for Lauderhill Homes — Built for Broward's Toughest Storms
intro:
Lauderhill sits at the heart of Broward County, a city of close-knit neighborhoods like Inverrary, Lauderhill Isles, and Sunrise Golf Village where families have built lives — and significant home equity — over decades. With average home values hovering around $280,000 and more than half the housing stock built before 1994, a large portion of Lauderhill's residential doors were never designed to meet the wind resistance standards that South Florida now demands. For homeowners in ZIP codes 33311, 33313, 33319, and 33351, upgrading to impact doors isn't a luxury — it's one of the most practical investments you can make in your property and your peace of mind.

Broward County's inland communities sometimes develop a false sense of security because they don't sit directly on the water. But at roughly 8 miles from the Atlantic coast, Lauderhill sits well within range of the destructive wind fields that accompany major hurricanes. The City of Lauderhill is rated for 170 mph design wind speeds under Florida Building Code — a threshold that standard hollow-core or even solid wood entry doors simply cannot meet. When a storm like Hurricane Wilma or Irma rolls through, it's often the entry points of a home — front doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors — that fail first, allowing catastrophic internal pressure changes that can lift roofs and destroy entire structures from the inside out.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has helped more than 500 homeowners across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties make this critical upgrade, and Lauderhill families are among those who stand to benefit the most. Our impact doors — sourced exclusively from trusted brands ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI — are installed entirely by our own in-house crews, never subcontracted. Whether you live near Central Broward Regional Park or in the quiet streets surrounding Inverrary Country Club, we bring the same meticulous craftsmanship and code-compliant expertise to every doorway we install.

section_why.h2: Why Lauderhill Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
Choosing the right contractor for impact door installation in Lauderhill isn't just about price — it's about trust, accountability, and knowing that the crew showing up to your home is the same team that stands behind the work for years to come. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing operates on a 100% in-house installation model, meaning we never hand your project off to third-party subcontractors. Every technician on our team is a direct employee who has been trained to our standards and who understands the specific permitting requirements enforced by the City of Lauderhill Building Division.

With more than 60 years of combined installation experience across our team, we've seen every door configuration, every framing condition, and every code challenge that Broward County can present. Older homes in neighborhoods like Lauderhill Isles and Sunrise Golf Village often come with non-standard door openings, aging concrete block frames, or pre-existing damage that a less experienced crew might overlook. Our installers assess each opening thoroughly before any door is ordered, ensuring the product we specify will fit correctly and perform exactly as rated when it matters most.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing holds an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau — a reflection of the transparency and follow-through we bring to every project. We're fully licensed and insured in the state of Florida, and we manage the entire permit process with the City of Lauderhill Building Division on your behalf, from submission through final inspection. You don't have to chase down inspectors or decode bureaucratic requirements. We handle it all.

We also understand that for Lauderhill's lower-middle income households, a major home improvement investment has to make financial sense. That's why we offer flexible financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options, making it realistic for families across the Lauderhill Mall Area and beyond to protect their homes without waiting years to save up. When you work with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not just getting a door — you're getting a partnership with a company that earns the name 'The Kings of Service' on every single job.

section_history.h2: Storm History That Proves Lauderhill Can't Afford to Wait
section_history.body:
Lauderhill has weathered nine named storms since 1990, and the damage patterns from three of them — Hurricane Andrew in 1992, Hurricane Wilma in 2005, and Hurricane Irma in 2017 — reshaped how Broward County residents think about home protection. Andrew, though it struck Miami-Dade most directly, sent wind bands powerful enough through Broward County to expose thousands of homes with inadequate door and window assemblies. The storm became the catalyst for the sweeping building code reforms that followed in 1994 — reforms that, unfortunately, came too late for the 55% of Lauderhill homes built before that year.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 hit Broward County particularly hard, making landfall near Cape Romano and tracking northeast in an unusual path that delivered sustained hurricane-force winds across the entire county for hours. Damage assessments following Wilma revealed a consistent pattern: homes whose entry doors and sliding glass doors had not been upgraded suffered the greatest interior destruction, as breached openings allowed wind and rain to pressurize interior spaces and compromise roof-to-wall connections. Neighborhoods throughout Lauderhill, including areas near Inverrary and Central Broward Regional Park, saw significant property losses tied directly to door failures.

Hurricane Irma in 2017 served as another stark reminder. Even as a storm that weakened before reaching Broward directly, Irma generated tornado-spawning outer bands that caused localized but severe damage throughout the county. For homeowners who had already installed impact doors, the difference was measurable: intact interiors, no flooding from wind-driven rain, and no structural compromise. For those who hadn't upgraded yet, the storm was another costly lesson. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing exists to ensure that Lauderhill homeowners don't have to learn that lesson the hard way.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Lauderhill
section_code.body:
Lauderhill falls under Broward County's jurisdiction for Florida Building Code compliance, and while the city is not classified as a High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation reserved for Miami-Dade and Broward's coastal-adjacent areas — it is still subject to stringent wind-resistance requirements that govern every door installation. The city's design wind speed of 170 mph means that all exterior doors, whether entry doors, sliding glass doors, or French doors, must be impact-rated or protected by approved storm shutters. For most Lauderhill homeowners, permanently installed impact doors are the far more practical and property-value-enhancing solution.

All impact door installations in Lauderhill require a permit issued through the City of Lauderhill Building Division. This permit process ensures that the products being installed carry the proper Notice of Acceptance (NOA) documentation demonstrating they have been tested and approved for Broward County's wind speed requirements. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles this permitting process end to end — we prepare the documentation, submit the application, coordinate with the Building Division, and schedule and manage all required inspections. Homeowners never have to navigate this process alone.

It's also important to understand that Florida Building Code prohibits mixing code-compliant products with non-compliant installation methods. A properly rated door installed incorrectly — with incorrect anchor spacing, inadequate frame reinforcement, or missing impact-rated hardware — will fail inspection and may perform poorly under storm conditions even if the door itself is rated. Our in-house installation teams are trained specifically to meet Broward County's code standards, ensuring your project passes inspection the first time and that your doors perform as rated when the next storm threatens the Lauderhill area.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premier-Grade Protection for Lauderhill Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors exclusively from two industry-leading manufacturers: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. These are not big-box retail products — they are commercial-grade, Florida Product Approved assemblies engineered specifically for South Florida's climate and code environment. For Lauderhill homeowners, this means every door we install has been tested against the large and small missile impact protocols required by Florida Building Code and carries the documentation to prove it.

ESW impact entry doors are among the most sought-after in Broward County for their combination of structural integrity and aesthetic versatility. Available in a range of panel designs, glass configurations, and finish options, ESW doors allow Lauderhill homeowners to upgrade their protection without sacrificing curb appeal — an important consideration when home values in the area average $280,000 and appearance directly affects resale potential. ESW doors feature multi-point locking systems that engage at three or more points along the door frame, dramatically increasing resistance to forced entry as well as wind load. CWI products bring similar performance with an emphasis on clean sightlines for sliding glass and French door applications, making them a popular choice for Lauderhill homes with rear patios, screened enclosures, or pool access points.

Both ESW and CWI product lines offer low-E insulating glass options that reduce heat transfer — a significant benefit in South Florida's subtropical climate. By blocking a meaningful portion of solar heat gain, these glass packages reduce the demand on your HVAC system, contributing to lower monthly energy bills year-round. When you combine storm protection, security, energy efficiency, and code compliance in a single product, the value proposition for Lauderhill homeowners becomes exceptionally clear.

section_cost.h2: Impact Door Costs and Financing Options for Lauderhill Families
section_cost.body:
For Lauderhill homeowners, understanding the real cost of impact door installation requires looking beyond the upfront price tag to consider what you're protecting, what you're gaining, and what you're avoiding. With average home values around $280,000, a properly installed set of impact doors typically represents between 2% and 5% of your home's value — a relatively modest investment that delivers returns through insurance savings, energy efficiency, added resale value, and the avoided cost of storm damage that can run into the tens of thousands of dollars in a single weather event.

The actual project cost for impact door installation in Lauderhill varies depending on the number of doors, the size of the openings, and the product configuration selected. A single impact entry door project typically ranges from $1,500 to $3,500 installed, while sliding glass door or French door replacements can range from $2,500 to $6,000 or more depending on width and glass package. Full-home door packages covering entry, side, and rear access points are often the most cost-effective approach. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides free, no-pressure in-home estimates that give Lauderhill families a clear and transparent picture of exactly what their project will cost before any commitment is made.

Because we know that a lower-middle income household can't always absorb a large home improvement cost at once, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available to qualified homeowners. This makes it entirely realistic to start protecting your Lauderhill home today rather than waiting for the next storm season to remind you of the risk. When you factor in the annual insurance premium savings that many Broward County homeowners see after installing impact doors — often ranging from $500 to $1,500 per year — the monthly financing payment can be partially or fully offset by what you stop paying your insurance company.

section_insurance.h2: How Impact Doors Lower Your Homeowners Insurance in Lauderhill
section_insurance.body:
Florida homeowners insurance has become one of the most significant recurring costs for property owners across Broward County, and Lauderhill residents are no exception. Premiums have climbed sharply in recent years as insurers recalibrate risk models in response to repeated storm losses across South Florida. One of the most effective tools Lauderhill homeowners have to push back against rising premiums is a Wind Mitigation Inspection — a formal assessment that documents the storm-resistant features of your home and can unlock meaningful discounts with most major carriers.

Impact-rated doors are among the opening protection upgrades that generate the highest credits on a wind mitigation report. Florida law requires insurance companies to offer premium discounts for verified wind mitigation features, and when all exterior openings of your home — including entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors — are protected by rated impact products, you may qualify for the highest available opening protection credit tier. Depending on your insurer and your current premium, Lauderhill homeowners with full opening protection have reported annual savings ranging from several hundred to over a thousand dollars on their windstorm coverage.

Common insurers serving the Lauderhill market, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Insurance, all recognize impact-rated door and window products in their mitigation credit structures. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all customers with the product documentation, NOA numbers, and installation records needed to support a wind mitigation inspection and insurance submission. We also recommend scheduling a new wind mitigation inspection with a certified inspector after your impact door installation is complete — it's the step that translates your investment into tangible, recurring insurance savings and makes your Lauderhill home a lower-risk property in the eyes of every carrier.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in Lauderhill if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Lauderhill is rated for 170 mph design wind speeds under Florida Building Code regardless of its distance from the coast. Hurricanes generate destructive wind fields that extend far inland, and Broward County's storm history — including damage from Wilma and Irma — demonstrates that inland homes face very real risk. Impact doors protect your home from wind pressure, wind-driven debris, and the internal pressurization that causes catastrophic structural damage during major storms.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost, and what financing does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offer?
faqs.a: Impact door installation in Lauderhill typically ranges from $1,500 to $6,000 or more per door depending on size, style, and glass configuration. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available to qualified homeowners. We provide free, transparent in-home estimates so you know exactly what your project will cost before you commit. When you factor in insurance savings, the net monthly cost is often lower than it first appears.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to install impact doors in Lauderhill, and how does that process work?
faqs.a: Yes, all impact door installations in Lauderhill require a permit from the City of Lauderhill Building Division. The products must carry Florida Product Approval documentation for Broward County's wind speed requirements. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permit process for you — from application and document preparation through scheduling and passing final inspection. You don't need to navigate the Building Division on your own; we handle it completely on your behalf.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take in Lauderhill?
faqs.a: Most single-door or small multi-door projects are completed in one day once the permit is approved and the products are in. Full-home door packages may take one to two days. The permitting and product lead time is typically two to four weeks from the date of your signed contract, depending on current volume and product availability. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing keeps customers informed throughout the process and works to schedule installation at your convenience.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors lower my homeowners insurance premium in Lauderhill?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. Florida law requires insurers to offer wind mitigation credits for verified storm-resistant features, and impact-rated doors covering all exterior openings qualify for the highest opening protection credit tier. Lauderhill homeowners with Citizens, Universal, Heritage, and other common carriers have reported annual savings ranging from several hundred to over a thousand dollars after a wind mitigation inspection confirms their impact door upgrades. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all documentation needed to support your updated wind mitigation report.
